data.title: Mao Geping Cosmetics Secures Shareholder Backing at 2025 AGM for Dividend and Governance Changes
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mao Geping Cosmetics Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1318) ) has provided an update.

Mao Geping Cosmetics Co., Ltd. held its 2025 annual general meeting in Hangzhou on April 24, 2026, with all nine board members, including chairman Mao Geping, attending in person and proceedings conducted by poll in compliance with PRC laws and Hong Kong listing rules. Shareholders and proxies representing about 68.65% of the company’s 490,186,900 issued H shares participated, with no shareholders required to abstain due to material interests, and Computershare Hong Kong Investor Services acting as scrutineer, paving the way for approval of the final dividend and amendments to the Articles of Association.

The AGM confirmed strong shareholder engagement, underscoring robust corporate governance and transparency as the company moves forward with changes to its Articles of Association. The endorsement of the final dividend signals management’s confidence in the business and delivers direct returns to investors, reinforcing Mao Geping Cosmetics’ commitment to shareholder value under its Hong Kong listing framework.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1318) stock is a Buy with a HK$130.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Mao Geping Cosmetics Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:1318 Stock Forecast page.

More about Mao Geping Cosmetics Co., Ltd. Class H

Mao Geping Cosmetics Co., Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed cosmetics company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China, focused on the beauty and personal care sector. The company’s H shares trade on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, and its governance follows PRC laws, Hong Kong listing rules and its Articles of Association.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

InnoCare Pharma Ltd. ( (HK:9969) ) has issued an update.

InnoCare Pharma plans to adopt a new 2026 RMB Share Incentive Scheme at its upcoming annual general meeting to strengthen its long-term incentive mechanisms and tie employees’ interests more closely to those of shareholders. The scheme aims to attract and retain key talent, motivate staff, and align core teams with the company’s long-term development.

Shareholders will be asked to approve the scheme, its mandate limit, related assessment measures, the issuance of new RMB shares under it, and authorization for the board to manage its implementation. The company has also secured a one-off waiver from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange so that RMB shares issued under the plan will not require listing in Hong Kong, with certain listing rule requirements modified to apply only to holders of Hong Kong-listed shares.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:9969) stock is a Buy with a HK$18.31 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on InnoCare Pharma Ltd. stock, see the HK:9969 Stock Forecast page.

InnoCare Pharma Limited is a biopharmaceutical company listed in Hong Kong that focuses on developing and commercializing innovative therapies. The company operates in both the Hong Kong and mainland China capital markets, issuing Hong Kong dollar and RMB-denominated shares to support its growth and incentivize its employees and core teams.

Cocoon Holdings Limited ( (HK:0428) ) has issued an announcement.

Cocoon Holdings Limited has convened its annual general meeting for 15 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the accompanying directors’ and auditor’s reports. The meeting will also consider the re-election of independent non-executive director Ms. Leung Yin Ting and executive director Mr. Chau Wai Hing, and authorise the board to determine directors’ remuneration for 2026.

Shareholders will vote on re-appointing McMillan Woods (Hong Kong) CPA Limited as independent auditor and empowering the board to set the auditor’s fees, reinforcing continuity in the company’s audit oversight. In addition, the board is seeking a general mandate to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s issued shares and a separate mandate to issue new shares and related instruments, measures that would give management greater flexibility in capital management and future fundraising while aligning with Hong Kong listing rules.

More about Cocoon Holdings Limited

Cocoon Holdings Limited is a company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and continued in Bermuda with limited liability, listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 428. It operates as an investment holding company, with its shares traded in the Hong Kong market and corporate governance overseen by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors.

data.description:

InnoCare Pharma Ltd. ( (HK:9969) ) has shared an update.

InnoCare Pharma has granted 15,389,245 restricted share units under its 2023 Share Award Scheme to three grantees, including executive directors Dr. Cui and Dr. Zhao and a senior manager, representing about 0.87% of its issued share capital. The RSUs, priced at nil with a reference share price of HK$14.7 on the grant date, will vest in four equal tranches over four years, subject to shareholder approval for awards to relevant executive directors.

The vesting of the RSUs is tied to performance targets at both corporate and individual levels, linking compensation to operating revenue milestones, the number of clinical trials and assessments of individual performance. The move reinforces InnoCare Pharma’s long-term incentive framework, aligning management interests with shareholders and signaling a focus on commercialization progress and R&D execution in the coming years.

data.title: Hanx Biopharmaceuticals Showcases Next-Generation Cancer Drugs at 2026 AACR Meeting
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hanx Biopharmaceuticals (Wuhan) Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3378) ) just unveiled an update.

Hanx Biopharmaceuticals announced that four of its innovative oncology research abstracts were presented as posters at the 2026 American Association for Cancer Research annual meeting in San Diego, underscoring the company’s scientific visibility and R&D capabilities. The presentations highlighted progress on two key assets: HX044, a globally innovative CTLA-4/SIRPα bifunctional fusion protein designed to enhance depletion of tumor-infiltrating regulatory T cells and potentially offer a wider therapeutic window than existing CTLA-4 therapies, and HX116, a potential first-in-class PD-L1xVEGF bispecific antibody-drug conjugate that showed superior preclinical anti-tumor activity and indications of more specific tumor targeting versus a leading PD-L1 ADC, reinforcing Hanx’s positioning in next-generation immuno-oncology and its prospects in the solid tumor treatment market.

More about Hanx Biopharmaceuticals (Wuhan) Co. Ltd. Class H

Hanx Biopharmaceuticals (Wuhan) Co., Ltd. is a China-based biopharmaceutical company focused on innovative oncology therapies. Its research and development pipeline centers on immuno-oncology biologics, including bispecific antibodies, antibody-drug conjugates and next-generation immune checkpoint modulators aimed at treating solid tumors with high unmet medical need.

China Chunlai Education Group Co., Ltd. ( (HK:1969) ) has provided an announcement.

China Chunlai Education Group, a Hong Kong-listed private higher education provider operating schools and affiliated entities in China, reported steady growth for the six months ended 28 February 2026. The group focuses on post-secondary education services, positioning itself within China’s expanding private education sector.

For the reporting period, the company recorded a 7.4% year-on-year increase in revenue to RMB956.3 million and a 5.7% rise in profit for the period to RMB429.8 million, with gross profit and profit before tax also improving. Management highlighted adjusted net profit, which grew 7.3%, as a non-IFRS performance measure used internally and for investors, while cautioning that this metric should complement, not replace, IFRS-based analysis and may not be directly comparable with peers’ figures.

Anhui Expressway Company ( (HK:0995) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Anhui Expressway Company has awarded procurement, supervision and testing contracts tied to the reconstruction and expansion of the G50 Shanghai–Chongqing Expressway section from Gezidun Junction to the Anhui–Hubei border. After an open tender, subsidiaries and associates of controlling shareholder Anhui Transportation Holding Group, including ATEGC, Transportation Material, Testing & Research Centre, Qixing Engineering and Zhongxing Supervision, were selected and related contracts were signed on 24 April 2026.

Because these counterparties are connected persons under Hong Kong listing rules, the arrangements constitute continuing connected transactions subject to disclosure, annual reporting and review requirements. The aggregate transaction size falls between 0.1% and 5% of the relevant percentage ratios, meaning independent shareholder approval is not required, but the deals formalize key supply and oversight roles for the G50 expansion project and underscore the group’s reliance on affiliated entities for critical materials and services.

An announcement from China Resources Pharmaceutical Group Ltd. ( (HK:3320) ) is now available.

China Resources Pharmaceutical Group has disclosed unaudited first-quarter 2026 figures for its Shenzhen-listed subsidiary, China Resources Boya Bio-pharmaceutical, which it controls with around 41.79% of the voting rights. The announcement highlights Boya’s role as a consolidated subsidiary within the wider group’s pharmaceutical operations, while noting that these figures do not provide a full picture of the group’s overall financial condition.

For the three months ended 31 March 2026, CR Boya reported revenue of RMB321.1 million and net profit of RMB30.0 million, both sharply down from the same period in 2025. The company also recorded a significant net decrease in cash and cash equivalents, despite maintaining solid owner’s equity of about RMB7.46 billion and reduced total liabilities, and shareholders are cautioned not to rely solely on this unaudited data when assessing the parent company’s securities.

An update from China Display Optoelectronics Technology Holdings Ltd ( (HK:0334) ) is now available.

China Display Optoelectronics Technology Holdings Ltd, a Bermuda-incorporated display and optoelectronics group listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, has called its annual general meeting for 28 May 2026 in Hong Kong. The company and its subsidiaries focus on display technologies and optoelectronic products for electronics industry customers.

At the annual general meeting, shareholders will be asked to adopt the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and reappoint Ernst & Young as auditors with board-authorised remuneration. The agenda also includes re-election of several executive and non-executive directors, approval for the board to set directors’ pay, and the grant of a general mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20 per cent of the company’s share capital, providing flexibility for future capital-raising and corporate actions.

More about China Display Optoelectronics Technology Holdings Ltd

China Display Optoelectronics Technology Holdings Ltd is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the display and optoelectronics sector. Through its subsidiaries, the group focuses on manufacturing and supplying display-related technologies and products for electronics and related markets.

data.title: Sinohealth Sets 2026 AGM to Approve Dividend, Board Mandates and Share Issue Authority
data.description:

Sinohealth Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:2361) ) has shared an announcement.

Sinohealth Technology Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for 18 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will vote on adopting the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and approving a final dividend of HK7.12 cents per share. Investors will also be asked to re-elect three directors, confirm Ernst & Young as auditor, and grant the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, moves that together shape capital management flexibility and refresh the group’s governance for the coming year.

The proposed dividend signals management’s willingness to return cash to shareholders based on 2025 performance, while the re-election of directors and auditor reaffirms continuity in oversight and financial reporting. The share issuance mandate, a common feature for Hong Kong-listed firms, would give Sinohealth additional capacity to raise equity or pursue strategic opportunities without convening separate meetings, potentially influencing its funding options and dilution risk for existing shareholders.

More about Sinohealth Holdings Ltd.

Sinohealth Technology Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 2361, operates in the technology sector serving the healthcare market. The company is overseen by a board of directors and engages an external auditor, reflecting a conventional listed-company governance and reporting structure oriented toward public-market investors.
